uncovered_waiver.reason: The flip is a pure additive wrapper: handle(request) calls the extracted _write_report() helper (byte-for-byte the pre-existing execute() body, unmodified in behavior — only moved into a shared private method so execute() and handle() perform the identical durable-write + event-emission sequence) and wraps its (run_path, run_id, report_data) tuple into ModelComplianceEvidenceOutput. This pool exercises both branches of the run_id `or` fallback and the scan_started_at `or` fallback (candidate 2), the event_bus-present publish path (all 3 candidates, a fixed bus/topic configuration is shared across the whole coverage-measured pool), and multi-node vs single-node vs zero-result report shapes. The event_bus=None / completion_topic='' branches inside _emit_completion (the 'no event bus configured' / 'no completion topic configured' log-only paths) are out of this pool's scope since handle()'s own construction always supplies both (a def-B EFFECT handler is runtime-wired with a real bus); those branches remain covered by the pre-existing test_readback_CATCHES_a_suppressed_effect canary via execute().
